functions.description: Initializes the embedded cRSegmentCache owner: a fixed 143x5 track-render BOD grid plus shared GDX cache vertex/index buffers and per-family scratch arrays. Its complete 0xa7f8-byte extent matches the Windows runtime size ledger.

functions.name: build_track_render_caches
functions.description: Builds the cRSegmentCache Floor, Slide, Warn, Ramp, and Fringe families from the owning SubgameRuntime’s fixed runtime-cell slab, using each embedded TrackRowCell anchor as the mesh-instance position.

functions.name: add_track_cache_vertex
functions.description: Transforms and deduplicates one source object vertex into a cRSegmentCache family staging buffer, optionally projecting track UVs.

functions.name: append_track_cache_object
functions.description: Appends one source object’s triangle or quad faces to a cRSegmentCache family by emitting deduplicated vertices and indices.

functions.name: update_track_render_cache_rows
functions.description: Advances cRSegmentCache while Goldy’s current row stays ahead of the next cached row, linking the Floor, Slide, Warn, Ramp, and Fringe BOD slots into their active lists and seeding each slot color.

functions.name: update_active_bod
functions.description: Implements the default active-bod subtype callback by recycling a live bod to the free list once its world-z plus the native 24-unit buffer falls behind the current subgame cutoff.

functions.name: remove_track_render_cache_bods
functions.description: Unlinks the cRSegmentCache owner’s fixed 143x5 BOD grid from the shared active-bod list during subgame teardown.

functions.name: bind_subgame_owner
functions.description: Binds a front-end controller’s first field to the embedded cRSubGame owner. For the 0x28-byte GUI receiver this is `cRGUI::Open()`; Windows folds the identical one-store body with the ThanksScreen owner-binding callsite.

functions.name: initialize_thanks_for_playing_screen
functions.description: Initializes the post-run Thanks For Playing owner, including the Splash backdrop, INTROTEXT.OGG, and the first shell-font message widget before state 30 begins updating it.

functions.name: update_thanks_for_playing_screen
functions.description: Runs the post-run Thanks For Playing message sequence, including the Challenge Mode and Time Trial prompts, Click to Continue, and the fade-out handoff back through the saved front-end return state.

functions.name: destroy_star_field
functions.description: Destroys the live star-field sprite pool, tears down its per-entry records, and returns the controller to state 0. Cross-port Android symbols match this exact member to `cRStarManager::UnInit()`.

functions.name: open_star_field
functions.description: Allocates the persistent star-field controller storage and, when the global star-pass flag is enabled, immediately initializes the live streak sprite pool for the requested slot count. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this exact member to `cRStarManager::Open(int)`.

functions.name: initialize_star_field
functions.description: Builds the live star-field streak sprites: allocates one sprite per entry, seeds camera-relative positions, randomizes direction and speed, tints them pale blue-white, and primes the per-streak length or fade fields. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this member to `cRStarManager::Init()`.

functions.name: hide_star_field
functions.description: Clears bit 0x40 on every live star-field sprite, hiding the background star pass without destroying its controller state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this exact member to `cRStarManager::Hide()`.

functions.name: unhide_star_field
functions.description: Sets bit 0x40 on every live star-field sprite, re-enabling the background streak pass without rebuilding the controller. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this exact member to `cRStarManager::UnHide()`.

functions.name: update_star_field
functions.description: Runs the four-state star-field controller, fading the streak sprites in or out around the global star-pass flag and dispatching the per-frame camera-relative position update. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this exact member to `cRStarManager::AI()`.

functions.name: update_star_positions
functions.description: Advances each star-field streak along its cached direction, resets wrapped entries against the current camera origin, and scales the visible sprite trail by both per-streak speed and the controller fade factor. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this member to `cRStarManager::UpdateStars(float)`.

functions.name: is_neighbor_cell_solid
functions.description: Exact Windows implementation of `cRSubGame::TestLoc(cRSubLoc*, int, int)`: derives the source row with SubLoc::Yi, applies lane and row offsets with bounds checks, probes the eight-lane runtime grid, rejects SubLoc::IsEmpty, then excludes tile ids 35, 0, 22, and 28. Android preserves the same method body and constants.

functions.name: build_track_fringe_objects
functions.description: Windows `cRSubGame::FringeEdgeTrack()`: allocates directional `Fringe` objects around runtime SubLoc cells for the post-build renderer, borrowing them from the embedded 7000-entry cRFringeManager pool at `data_4df904 + 0x3d01d4`. Android preserves the owner and method name.

functions.name: merge_track_tile_runs
functions.description: Collapses repeated horizontal tile runs into longer strip variants where the renderer allows it.

functions.name: mark_track_warning_zones
functions.description: Marks warning-zone footprints around hazard-bearing runtime tiles before the fringe-object and cache builders run. Cross-port Android symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::WarnTrack()`.

functions.name: harmonize_center_lane_floor_slide_variants
functions.description: Normalizes center-lane floor and slide seam variants after the main edge pass. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::SlideSmoothTrack()`.

functions.name: select_track_tile_edge_variants
functions.description: Computes neighbor masks and chooses edge or corner variants for special runtime tile classes. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::SmoothTrack()`.

functions.name: build_track_colours
functions.description: Builds the active subgame track colour banks used by skirt, fringe, and row-cache rendering. Cross-port iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::BuildColours()` in `SubGame.o`.

functions.name: set_subgame_features
functions.description: Derives the active subgame feature flags from the current mode, level, and challenge setup before runtime rows and pickups are built. Cross-port iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::SetFeatures()` in `SubGame.o`.

functions.name: populate_runtime_track_cells_from_segments
functions.description: Copies authored segment rows into the generated runtime grid, seeds Goldy's visible life stock to 3 before `initialize_subgoldy` runs, and seeds the course row bounds: non-random levels use the final `Last:` block boundary while the mode-1 random branch keeps the authored `Length:` lane scaled by the challenge scalar before subtracting the final `Last:` block rows.

functions.name: initialize_subgame
functions.description: Initializes the active gameplay subgame state, including the continuation selector at `+0x1270fc8`, per-mode HUD widgets, replay-backed startup, and the first galaxy, challenge-setup, or gameplay handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::Init()`.

functions.name: update_progress_bar
functions.description: Exact `cRProgressBar::AI()` method for the empty authored class at Player +0x3f0. It ignores `this`, derives track progress from the containing Goldy's z position and SubgameRuntime row bounds, and queues the three left-column HUD quads. Android and iOS preserve the same owner role.

functions.name: rebuild_track_runtime_from_segments
functions.description: Normalizes the generated runtime grid, copies authored metadata into runtime cells, and runs the post-build render passes.

functions.name: build_subgame_level
functions.description: Builds the active subgame course, including level-file load, runtime track rebuild, mode-specific HUD setup, backdrop selection, and the first gameplay object lists. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::BuildLevel()`.

functions.name: complete_subgame
functions.description: Snapshots the just-finished run into the working high-score/replay record, including the normalized garbage and salt frequencies, increments the per-run completion counter, and dispatches the mode-specific high-score handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::Complete(bool)`.

functions.name: destroy_subgame
functions.description: Tears down the active gameplay subgame state, including runtime lists, HUD widgets, and the current mode handoff state before control returns to the front-end. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_subgame
functions.description: Runs the main gameplay subgame state machine, including the continuation-controlled galaxy or challenge-setup handoff, replay-backed level start or restart, pause and quit flow, HUD timing, camera updates, active gameplay tick, and forward runtime-row scan for pickups plus authored or ambient garbage/salt hazards. Generated garbage/salt spawns are suppressed while the live `Player::click_start.state` lane at +0x120 equals 2. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::AI()`.

functions.name: is_slide_cache_tile_family
functions.description: Exact SubLoc predicate matching cross-port `cRSubLoc::IsFloor()`: accepts tile ids 1, 20, 21, 33, 34, and 27. The retained Windows harness name describes its render-cache use.

functions.name: is_ramp_cache_tile_family
functions.description: Exact SubLoc predicate matching cross-port `cRSubLoc::IsRamp()`: accepts the authored ramp tile family 2 through 13.

functions.name: is_open_neighbor_tile_family
functions.description: Exact SubLoc predicate matching cross-port `cRSubLoc::IsEmpty()`: accepts tile ids 0, 28, 35, 14, and 29. The retained Windows name describes its neighbor-test use.

functions.name: is_floor_cache_tile_family
functions.description: Exact SubLoc predicate matching Android `cRSubLoc::IsSlide()`: accepts tile ids 15, 23, 24, 25, 26, 16, 18, and 19. The historical Windows cache-family name is opposite the authored IsFloor helper at 0x439a40.

functions.name: refresh_fringe_object_draw_list
functions.description: Windows cRFringe callback: copies the current track skirt tint into one authored Fringe and, once its world-z falls behind the live fringe threshold, unlinks it from the active list and requeues it onto the shared draw-list bucket. The constructor table points directly here; Android preserves `cRFringe::AI()` but implements it as a no-op.

functions.name: destroy_sub_lazer_projectile
functions.description: Windows `cRSubLoc::Remove()`: tears down one live SubLoc by removing its own BOD, clearing the attachment-row model for entry tiles when required, and unlinking its four borrowed fringe BODs. Android preserves the authored name and the same Yi/row-body/own-body lifecycle; the stable Windows harness name is historical.

functions.name: wall2_emitter_maybe_fire_sub_lazer
functions.description: Updates one runtime `TrackRowCell`/`cRSubLoc`. Wall2 tile 14 fires through the owned 20-slot `SubLazerPool` after `first_block_row_count` passes Goldy's z; tiles 22/29/30 and the default path cull against the embedded player's interaction plane, while skirt tiles refresh `runtime_rows[row].attachment_body.color`. Cross-port iOS names the owner family `cRSubLoc::AI()`.

functions.name: health_collect_particles
functions.description: Exact `Player` collection burst for one `SubHealth`, emitting eight particles from the pickup sprite. Android and iOS retain the authored member as `cRSubGoldy::HealthCollect(cRSubHealth*)`.

functions.name: update_player_movement_flags
functions.description: Maps the player movement-flag selector at +0x308 onto movement_flags and the movement-fire progress step at +0x2734, then refreshes the 0x2984 state machine when the mask changes.

functions.name: update_movement_flag_emitters
functions.description: Iterates the 12 Golb emitter slots at player+0x450 and seeds one, two, or three live shot actors based on the current movement_flags family.

functions.name: end_jetpack_hover
functions.description: Exact Windows `SubHover::end_jetpack_hover`, authored as `cRSubHover::End`: while the exact 0x214-byte owner is active, clamps progress to the 0.94 teardown threshold.

functions.name: update_jetpack_gauge
functions.description: Windows `SubHover::update_jetpack_gauge`, authored as `cRSubHover::AI`: advances the hover countdown/warning envelope, applies runtime-row auto-shutoff, drives wobble and the authored Hover call, and clears the exact 0x214-byte owner at teardown.

functions.name: uninit_jet_particles
functions.description: Exact Windows `SubHover::uninit_jet_particles`, authored as `cRSubHover::JetUnInit`: kills both sprites in each of the owner's fifteen inline jet rows.

functions.name: initialize_jet_particles
functions.description: Exact Windows `SubHover::initialize_jet_particles`, authored as `cRSubHover::JetInit`: allocates and initializes the 15-by-2 inline jet sprite grid.

functions.name: update_jet_particles
functions.description: Windows `SubHover::update_jet_particles`, authored as `cRSubHover::Jets`: projects the owner's 15-by-2 jet exhaust grid backward from the authored snail hotspots and occasionally emits a detached puff with 85% of Player velocity.

functions.name: initialize_jetpack_gauge
functions.description: Exact Windows `SubHover::initialize_jetpack_gauge`, authored as `cRSubHover::Init`: clears the 0x214-byte child state, seeds its 1/600 progress step, and stores borrowed SubgameRuntime and Player backlinks. The authored contract is void; the Windows integer parameter is unused.

functions.name: arm_jetpack_gauge
functions.description: Exact Windows `SubHover::arm_jetpack_gauge`, authored as `cRSubHover::On`: arms the child at Player +0x2750, clears progress and wobble, selects the snail jetpack visual, and calls JetInit. The authored contract is void.

functions.name: initialize_subgoldy
functions.description: Authored void `cRSubGoldy::Init(int)`: initializes the SubgameRuntime-owned Goldy actor, including movement/follow state, animation and cutscene owners, cached camera target, exact cRSubHover child, cameraman, and click-start prompt. Android and iOS retain the method.

functions.name: show_subgoldy_lives
functions.description: Refreshes the nine-slot Goldy life display by showing the first `current_lives` widgets and hiding the rest. Windows `update_subgoldy` only calls it for gameplay mode 0, matching the mode-gated `cRSubGoldy::ShowLives()` helper in the Android and iOS ports.

functions.name: begin_post_follow_carryover
functions.description: Begins Goldy's pending post-follow carryover window. If the active follow byte is still set, it copies `follow_state.orientation_b` into `post_follow_exit_roll` and the live attachment record's installed-heading bits into `post_follow_heading_carryover`, clears `follow_state.active`, sets `attachment_exit_pending`, latches player z into `attachment_exit_anchor_z`, and zeroes the exit-progress and gate bytes. Windows `cdb` confirmed this helper can also run after the active follow byte has already been cleared, so it is one real exit lane but not the only attachment-retirement path.

functions.name: play_movement_state_sound
functions.description: Chooses one of three movement sound families from movement_flags and attenuates playback while attachment-exit handling is active.

functions.name: update_subgoldy
functions.description: Main per-frame player or Goldy actor step. Updates attachment-follow state, samples current track cells, drives row events, dispatches pickups and completion handoff, emits movement flags and sounds, and advances the runtime track index. It contains four direct calls to `begin_post_follow_carryover`, plus the post-exit progress loop that advances `attachment_exit_progress` by `attachment_exit_progress_step` and trips the `gate_a` and `gate_b` thresholds. Windows `cdb` confirmed those carryover-arm lanes are real, but at least one clean level-complete path bypasses all four of them. functions.name: update_cutscene
functions.description: Authored `cRCutScene::AI()`: advances the exact embedded CutScene, consuming live snail hotspots for intro/completion/death camera anchors, including the `CameraSkidStop -> CameraIntroTalk` blend and completion-screen handoff.

functions.name: initialize_subgoldy_death
functions.description: Chooses Goldy's death outcome from the current gameplay mode and visible life stock, then enters the resurrect state machine in either respawn or final-loss mode. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::DeathInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_warning
functions.description: Initializes the exact 0x10-byte Windows cRWarning owner embedded in Player at +0x3f4: allocates and hides its border, then resets the phase state. Android and iOS retain cRWarning::Init, with wider mobile presentation layouts.

functions.name: uninit_warning
functions.description: Releases the border owned by Player's embedded cRWarning. Android preserves this lifecycle edge as cRWarning::UnInit().

functions.name: start_warning
functions.description: Starts the embedded cRWarning fade cycle and unhides its border. Android cRDamageGuage::AI() calls cRWarning::Start() through the same owner relationship.

functions.name: stop_warning
functions.description: Stops the embedded cRWarning state machine and hides its border. Android and iOS retain this method as cRWarning::Stop().

functions.name: stop_warning_sample
functions.description: cRWarning::StopSample ownership recovered from Android and iOS. The Windows method stops the warning sample through the shared sound manager without reading its receiver; Android's port-specific body is a no-op.

functions.name: update_warning
functions.description: Advances Player's embedded cRWarning phase and warning-border alpha while gameplay is unpaused, alternating its solid and fade states and replaying the warning cue. Android and iOS retain the same owner role as cRWarning::AI().

functions.name: get_track_cell_row_index
functions.description: Exact Windows implementation of `cRSubLoc::Yi()`: masks the cell's low three lane bits, backs up to lane zero, subtracts the runtime-cell slab base, and divides by eight cells per row. The iOS implementation independently preserves the same algorithm with its port-specific 0x44-byte cell stride; Windows uses 0x54.

functions.name: initialize_fringe_manager
functions.description: Resets the shared fringe-manager pool counter at `data_4df904 + 0x3d01d4` before one track rebuild emits directional fringe objects. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRFringeManager::Init()`.

functions.name: allocate_fringe_object
functions.description: Allocates the next 0x38-byte object from the shared 7000-entry fringe-manager pool, or raises the `Too many Fringes` error if the pool overflows. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRFringeManager::GetFringe()`.

functions.name: uninit_nuke
functions.description: Kills the 25 sprite slots owned by the exact 0x7c-byte cRNuke collision-ring effect at Player +0x150 and clears its active state. Android and iOS retain this method as `cRNuke::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_nuke
functions.description: Initializes the exact 0x7c-byte cRNuke owner at Player +0x150: spawns the 25-sprite ring effect used by collision kinds `2` and `6`, seeds its orbit height and phase step from the owning player and global tick delta, and immediately dispatches `cRNuke::AI()`. Android retains this method as `cRNuke::Init()`.

functions.name: update_nuke
functions.description: Advances the exact cRNuke owner by moving its 25 sprite slots around the containing player at radius `7.0`, using the +0x08/+0x0c z-step pair and +0x10/+0x14 phase pair. Android and iOS retain this method as `cRNuke::AI()`.

functions.name: recycle_bod_to_free_list
functions.description: Exact emitted `cLinkedList<cRBod>::Remove` helper: unlinks one active BOD, moves it onto the shared free-list head, and diagnoses invalid ownership or active-iteration removal. The same owned `BodList` body is inlined by pickup AI teardown paths.

functions.name: copy_segment_definition_to_level_slot
functions.description: Finds a segment catalog entry by its enumerated filename, transposes its column-major eight-lane glyph grid into a Windows SubSegment, and copies the authored row metadata including position and velocity vectors, path template, and ring-speed fields. The symbol-preserving iOS counterpart is the void member `cRSubTracks::ImportSegment(char*, cRSubSegment*)`; Windows staticizes the catalog lookup through the root owner and stores the 0x4220-byte SubSegment payload inline.

functions.name: load_level_definition_file
functions.description: Parses one Levels/*.txt definition into the owning SubTracks instance, including its SubSegments, display metadata, speed, hazard frequencies, landscape, parcel, and texture fields. Symbol-preserving iOS builds name the corresponding void overload `cRSubTracks::Init(char*)`; all Windows callers discard the path-dependent incidental value left in EAX.

functions.name: load_builtin_segment_definitions
functions.description: Initializes the secondary SubTracks owner from the shipped `SubSegmentRaw*` table at `0x4a63d0`, including records such as `Start`, `Finish`, and `Filler` plus their eight authored glyph rows and path metadata. The inconsistent incidental return register proves the Windows member is void; symbol-preserving iOS builds name the corresponding overload `cRSubTracks::Init(cRSubSegmentRaw**)`.

functions.name: load_segment_definitions
functions.description: Implements the void `cRSMTracks::Import()` member: parses SEGMENTS/*.TXT into the exact 0x25cfb4-byte SMTracks owner, stores the internal `Name:'...'` display label separately from the enumerated filename, and recovers per-row parcel, model, velocity, path, ring, ring-speed, and jetpack-off metadata.

functions.name: load_level_definitions
functions.description: Implements the void `cRSMTracks::OpenLevels()` member by enumerating LEVELS/*.TXT and parsing each name into the root-owned SubTracks scratch definition.

functions.name: format_time_trial_string
functions.description: Implements `cRTimeTrial::TimeString(cRTime&)`: formats one authored Time/cRTime value into the shared Time Trial HUD buffer. The receiver is the exact 0x330-byte TimeTrial owner embedded at SubgameRuntime +0xff25e0, although the body does not read receiver fields.

functions.name: kill_tip_widgets
functions.description: Kills the live border widgets owned by one gameplay tip slot, including the main tip body and any optional OK or Disable buttons, then clears the tip's active flag.

functions.name: initialize_tip
functions.description: Builds one gameplay tip slot, including the main border widget, optional OK or Disable buttons, and the timed auto-dismiss rate for transient tips.

functions.name: update_tip
functions.description: Runs one gameplay tip slot, handling hide or show state, button-click consumption, timed expiry, and the final teardown or UI-state restore path.

functions.name: enqueue_tip_message
functions.description: Allocates one of the three small gameplay tip-message slots, seeds it with the requested text payload, and warns if all slots are already occupied.

functions.name: initialize_tutorial
functions.description: Exact Windows `Tutorial::initialize_tutorial`: seeds the embedded 0x1c-byte tutorial owner, borrows its containing SubgameRuntime, and applies the tutorial runtime flags. Android and iOS retain `cRTutorial::Init()`.

functions.name: uninit_tutorial
functions.description: Exact Windows `Tutorial::uninit_tutorial` thunk that tears down the root tip manager for tutorial-mode exit. Android and iOS retain `cRTutorial::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_tutorial
functions.description: Exact Windows `Tutorial::update_tutorial` thunk: samples the live Goldy position through the borrowed containing SubgameRuntime. Android and iOS retain `cRTutorial::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_bass_audio_backend
functions.description: Extracts Bass.dll from game data into tBass.dll, loads the BASS 2.0 exports dynamically, and initializes the audio backend.

functions.name: uninitialize_bass_audio_backend
functions.description: Stops the global BASS backend, frees the loaded `Bass.dll` state, unloads the temporary DLL, and deletes the extracted `tBass.dll` copy.

functions.name: ensure_music_stream_from_path
functions.description: Ensures the requested music path is the active BASS stream, skipping a restart when the cached path already matches and otherwise stopping any previous stream before opening and playing the new path.

functions.name: prepare_music_stream_reload_if_path_changed
functions.description: Checks whether the requested music path differs from the cached active path and, when it does, stops the current music stream so `cache_music_file` can reload it from archive bytes.

functions.name: play_music_stream_from_bytes
functions.description: Stops any previous active music stream, opens a new BASS stream from already-read music bytes, and starts playback while caching the source path.

functions.name: stop_music_stream
functions.description: Stops the active BASS music stream, removes any registered sync callback, frees the stream handle, and clears the active-stream flag.

functions.name: load_registered_sound_sample_from_path
functions.description: Loads one sample directly from a filesystem path into the shared runtime sound-bank slot table via `BASS_SampleLoad(mem=0, ...)`, storing the resulting sample handle at the requested registered-sample index.

functions.name: load_registered_sound_sample_from_bytes
functions.description: Loads one sample from already-read archive bytes into the shared runtime sound-bank slot table via `BASS_SampleLoad(mem=1, ...)`, storing the resulting sample handle at the requested registered-sample index.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_scaled
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample through the BASS sample backend with the supplied normalized gain scale and the default extra playback parameters.

functions.name: stop_sound_sample_handle
functions.description: Forwards one raw runtime sample handle directly into `BASS_SampleStop`, underneath the registered-sample and warning-owned stop wrappers.

functions.name: stop_registered_sound_sample
functions.description: Stops playback for one registered runtime sample index by resolving it through the shared sound-bank table before forwarding into the lower audio backend.

functions.name: is_registered_sound_sample_playing
functions.description: Returns whether one registered runtime sample index is currently playing by querying the shared sound-bank table entry in the lower audio backend.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_default
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample index through the default `BASS_SamplePlayEx` wrapper with the hard-coded playback parameters used by the warning-owned sample path.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_backend
functions.description: Forwards one registered runtime sample index plus normalized playback scalars into the raw `BASS_SamplePlayEx` lane that the SFX and voice wrappers share.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_scaled_panned
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample through the BASS sample backend with the supplied normalized gain scale and explicit pan override.

functions.name: set_global_sample_volume_config
functions.description: Scales one normalized [0,1] sample-volume scalar onto BASS config option `4` (`BASS_CONFIG_GVOL_SAMPLE`) before the backend stores it as the live global SFX sample volume.

functions.name: set_global_stream_volume_config
functions.description: Scales one normalized [0,1] stream-volume scalar onto BASS config option `5` (`BASS_CONFIG_GVOL_STREAM`) before the backend stores it as the live music or stream volume.

functions.name: stop_audio_backend
functions.description: Forwards directly into the global BASS stop export to halt active backend playback during shutdown or full audio resets.

functions.name: resume_audio_backend_if_paused
functions.description: Restarts the global BASS backend only when the audio state flag at `backend + 0x18` says the backend is currently paused, then clears that paused flag.

functions.name: pause_audio_backend_if_running
functions.description: Pauses the global BASS backend only when the audio state flag at `backend + 0x18` says the backend is currently running, then marks it paused.

functions.name: set_audio_normalization_scales
functions.description: Stores the normalized music, SFX, and voice gain scales parsed from `VOICE/_VOICE.TXT` into the shared backend lanes that the music, sound, and voice wrappers multiply into live playback volume.

functions.name: initialize_font_wave_state
functions.description: Seeds the shared sine-wave text animation phases and per-tick phase increments that `draw_font_text_instance` consumes for wobble text.

functions.name: update_font_wave_state
functions.description: Advances the shared sine-wave text animation phases, wraps them into `[0, 2pi)`, and resets the transient font-wave bookkeeping lanes each frame.

functions.name: font_slot_index_for_char
functions.description: Maps front-end text bytes onto FONT-MENU-HOVER atlas slot indices; shipped tutorial strings reuse bytes like `{`, `}`, `[`, `]`, `~`, `_`, `^`, and `;` as icon tokens.

functions.name: measure_font_text_width
functions.description: Accumulates atlas slot widths for one string using the registered font's spacing scalar and trailing space padding.

functions.name: register_font_texture_sheet
functions.description: Loads one FONT-MENU-HOVER atlas, scans row-0 white delimiters and the column-0 height marker, and registers per-slot metrics and texture refs.

functions.name: draw_font_text_instance
functions.description: Draws one queued font text instance by mapping each byte through the atlas slot table and submitting textured glyph quads.

functions.name: draw_queued_font_quad_instance
functions.description: Draws one queued explicit font-quad record by forwarding its stored coordinates, UVs, size mode, angle, and color into the shared immediate textured-quad helper.

functions.name: draw_font_text_queue
functions.description: Iterates the live font text queue, retires dead entries, and draws the ones matching the supplied render mask.

functions.name: queue_font_text_instance
functions.description: Copies one text run into the shared font print string buffer and appends the matching queued font-text instance record with its font, position, alignment, colors, and render flags.

functions.name: queue_axis_aligned_textured_quad
functions.description: Appends one axis-aligned textured quad with default `[0,1]` UVs into the shared 2D render queue, using the supplied texture, authored rectangle, tint, and render flags.

functions.name: queue_axis_aligned_textured_quad_uv
functions.description: Appends one axis-aligned textured quad with explicit UV bounds into the shared 2D render queue. Front-end widgets, progress bars, and the damage gauge all use this helper.

functions.name: queue_textured_quad_corners
functions.description: Appends one arbitrary four-corner textured quad with explicit per-corner positions and UV bounds into the shared 2D render queue. The backdrop and galaxy-line renderers use this helper.

functions.name: layout_and_queue_wrapped_font_text
functions.description: Splits one text string into wrapped line runs, queues each run through `queue_font_text_instance`, and returns the aligned authored-space origin and measured bounds for the wrapped block.

functions.name: initialize_font3d_objects
functions.description: Builds the per-font `Objects/Font3D` bod instances, applies the atlas UV layout for each glyph slot, and scales each object to the registered font metrics.

functions.name: query_directx_runtime_version
functions.description: Dynamically probes DirectDraw, DirectInput, and Direct3D 8 availability and returns the highest DirectX-style runtime version code reached by the startup capability check.

functions.name: append_enumerated_input_controller_callback
functions.description: DirectInput device-enumeration callback that creates one joystick device from the reported instance, appends it to the global controller list, and stops enumeration once four devices are present.

functions.name: configure_input_controller_axis_range_callback
functions.description: DirectInput object-enumeration callback that applies the native `[-1000, 1000]` range property to each axis object on one enumerated input controller.

functions.name: update_joystick_input
functions.description: Polls each enumerated DirectInput joystick device, reacquires on transient loss, normalizes its primary X/Y axes into the shared [-1000, 1000] range, packs the sampled buttons into the game's bitmask format, and publishes the result through the input-controller slot writer.

functions.name: release_input_controllers
functions.description: Releases every enumerated DirectInput controller device plus the shared DirectInput interface and clears the global controller list.

functions.name: initialize_keyboard_input
functions.description: Initializes the DirectInput keyboard device, sets its data format/cooperative level, acquires it, and clears the previous/current key-state buffers.

functions.name: update_keyboard_input
functions.description: Polls the DirectInput keyboard device into the current and previous 0x100-byte key-state tables, retries reacquisition on transient failures, and publishes the derived digital input flags and directional axes into the shared input-controller lanes.

functions.name: is_key_pressed_edge
functions.description: Returns true when the supplied keyboard scancode is down in the current DirectInput key-state table and was up in the previous frame's table.

functions.name: is_key_down
functions.description: Returns true when the supplied keyboard scancode is down in the current DirectInput key-state table.

functions.name: release_keyboard_input
functions.description: Releases the DirectInput keyboard device and its shared DirectInput interface, clearing the global keyboard-input handles.

functions.name: initialize_mouse_authored_scale_from_clip_rect
functions.description: Caches the current clipped screen rectangle and initializes the shared mouse screen-to-authored scaling for the default 640x480 authored coordinate space.

functions.name: update_mouse_authored_scale
functions.description: Computes the shared mouse screen-to-authored X/Y scale factors from the cached clip rectangle and the supplied authored width and height. convert_mouse_screen_xy consumes those scales.

functions.name: resolve_uncaptured_cursor_sensitivity_scale
functions.description: Returns the cursor-delta sensitivity scale for uncaptured pointer mode: 1.0 while the cursor is captured, otherwise arg * 1.8 + 0.7.

functions.name: set_hide_system_cursor_flag
functions.description: Sets the shared latch that tells `update_mouse` whether to hide the OS cursor after the current pointer-region update.

functions.name: click_mouse_screen
functions.description: Optionally forwards a cursor position to SetCursorPos when the window is active and captured, then stores raw/live mouse coordinates for the selected slot and publishes authored x/y through player zero's borrowed cRGameInput owner.

functions.name: release_mouse_input
functions.description: Releases the DirectInput mouse device and its shared DirectInput interface, clearing the global mouse-input handles.

functions.name: is_mouse_captured
functions.description: Returns the active byte at the head of the player-owned cRMouse-compatible subobject stored at GameRoot +0x290; iOS names the same method cRMouse::IsActive().

functions.name: capture_mouse_cursor
functions.description: Implements the Windows cRMouse::SetActive() lane at GameRoot +0x290: snapshots live x/y, warps controller zero through click_mouse_screen, marks the one-frame draw suppression latch, and enables the active byte.

functions.name: release_mouse_cursor
functions.description: Implements cRMouse::SetInActive() for the player-owned mouse subobject by restoring saved x/y into the live lanes when active and clearing byte zero.

functions.name: load_object_definition
functions.description: Parses one authored object definition into the caller object; iOS RObject.o names this `cRObject::Load(char*)`.

functions.name: initialize_global_identity_matrix
functions.description: Startup wrapper that seeds the shared matrix at `data_77ff40` by calling `initialize_uniform_scale_matrix(..., 1.0)`. It is only referenced from the CRT constructor table.

functions.name: convert_math_type32_to_16
functions.description: Converts one float into the shared signed-16 gameplay math representation by scaling with `65536 / scale` and truncating. Android symbols match this helper to `MathType32to16(float, float)`, and both Android and Windows only xref it from the SubGoldy update path.

functions.name: convert_math_type16_to_32
functions.description: Converts one signed-16 gameplay math value back into float form by scaling with `scale / 65536`. Android symbols match this helper to `MathType16to32(short, float)`, and both Android and Windows only xref it from the SubGoldy update path.

functions.name: initialize_math_random_table
functions.description: Fills the shared 0x1fff-entry gameplay random table from the underlying linear-congruential generator and resets the ring index. Android symbols match this helper family to `gRMathRand2Init()`.

functions.name: next_math_random_value
functions.description: Advances the shared gameplay random-table index modulo 0x1fff and returns the next cached 15-bit value. Android symbols match this helper family to the shared `Rand()` / `gRMathRand2()` accessors.

functions.name: initialize_trigonometry_tables
functions.description: Builds the shared cosine and sine lookup tables used by the gameplay math wrappers during startup, then resets the companion random table state.

functions.name: set_math_random_seed
functions.description: Stores one caller-provided seed into the shared gameplay linear-congruential generator before the random table is consumed. Android symbols match this helper family to `RandSeed(int)`.

functions.name: cosine
functions.description: Computes one cosine value through the shared gameplay trigonometry wrapper. Android symbols match this helper to `Cos(float)`.

functions.name: sine
functions.description: Computes one sine value through the shared gameplay trigonometry wrapper. Android symbols match this helper to `Sin(float)`.

functions.name: arccosine
functions.description: Computes the arccosine of one float input. Android symbols match this helper to `ACos(float)`.

functions.name: atan2_positive
functions.description: Computes a two-argument arctangent with the result wrapped into the positive full-turn range. Android symbols match this helper to `ATan(float, float)`.

functions.name: square_root
functions.description: Computes the square root of one float input. Android symbols match this helper to `Sqrt(float)`.

functions.name: multiply_vector_by_matrix_copy
functions.description: Writes an out-of-place affine vector-by-matrix transform into a destination output. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::operator*(const tMatrix&) const`.

functions.name: dot_vectors
functions.description: Computes the 3D dot product of two vectors. Android symbols match this helper to the static overload `tVector::Dot(const tVector&, const tVector&)`.

functions.name: dot_vector
functions.description: Computes the 3D dot product of one vector against another through the member-style overload `tVector::Dot(const tVector&)`.

functions.name: multiply_vector_by_matrix
functions.description: Applies one affine matrix to a vector in place, including translation. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Multiply(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: rotate_vector_by_matrix
functions.description: Applies only the 3x3 rotational basis of a matrix to a vector in place, leaving translation out. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Rotate(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: normalize_vector_from_source
functions.description: Copies a source vector and normalizes it in place. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Normalize(const tVector&)`.

functions.name: cross_vectors
functions.description: Computes the 3D cross product of two vectors into a destination output. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Cross(const tVector&, const tVector&)`.

functions.name: initialize_uniform_scale_matrix
functions.description: Initializes a matrix from one scalar by writing the same value across the xyz diagonal, zeroing the off-diagonal and translation slots, and setting homogeneous `w` to 1.0. Android symbols match this helper to the single-float `tMatrix::tMatrix(float)` constructor.

functions.name: multiply_matrices
functions.description: Core affine matrix multiply that writes `out = lhs * rhs` for the 3x3 basis and translated origin row. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Multiply(const tMatrix&, const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: set_matrix_identity
functions.description: Resets one full 4x4 transform matrix to identity, including translation. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Identity()`.

functions.name: set_matrix_rotation_identity
functions.description: Resets the rotational basis and homogeneous row to identity while preserving the translation slots. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::RotIdentity()`.

functions.name: invert_matrix_in_place
functions.description: Inverts one orthonormal transform matrix in place by transposing the basis and recomputing the negated translated origin. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Invert()`.

functions.name: invert_matrix_from_source
functions.description: Writes the inverse of a source orthonormal transform into a destination matrix. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Invert(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: initialize_quaternion_from_axis
functions.description: Builds a quaternion from an axis-angle record by applying `sin(angle / 2)` to the xyz axis and `cos(angle / 2)` to `w`. Android symbols match this helper to `tQuaternian::tQuaternian(const tAxis&)`.

functions.name: initialize_axis_from_quaternion
functions.description: Converts a quaternion into axis-angle form by normalizing xyz against `sin(theta / 2)` and storing `angle = 2 * acos(w)`. Android symbols match this helper to `tAxis::operator=(const tQuaternian&)`.

functions.name: initialize_quaternion_from_matrix
functions.description: Builds a quaternion from the rotational basis of a transform matrix. Android symbols match this helper to `tQuaternian::tQuaternian(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: initialize_matrix_from_quaternion
functions.description: Builds a rotation matrix from a quaternion and resets translation and homogeneous slots to their identity values. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::tMatrix(const tQuaternian&)`.

functions.name: linear_interpolate_matrix
functions.description: Interpolates one transform toward another by blending rotation in matrix space and linearly blending translation. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::LinearInterpolate(const tMatrix&, const tMatrix&, float)`.

functions.name: set_color_rgba
functions.description: Stores one four-float RGBA color tuple into the destination color/vector slot and returns the same destination pointer.

functions.name: set_color_alpha
functions.description: Stores one float alpha value into the destination color slot's alpha lane and returns that same alpha value.

functions.name: set_color_grayscale
functions.description: Stores one uniform grayscale RGB value into the destination color slot, forces alpha to `1.0`, and returns the grayscale input.

functions.name: store_color4f
functions.description: Stores one four-float tuple into the destination `Color4f` slot and returns the third component register value left live by the helper epilogue. Callers treat it as a write-only color helper.

functions.name: pack_color_rgba_u8
functions.description: Converts one four-float RGBA tuple in `[0,1]` into one packed BGRA8 color record for sprite and mesh draw paths.

functions.name: set_color_white
functions.description: Thin wrapper around `set_color_grayscale` that initializes one destination color slot to opaque white.

functions.name: set_color_black
functions.description: Thin wrapper around `set_color_grayscale` that initializes one destination color slot to opaque black.

functions.name: random_signed_float_below
functions.description: Returns a centered random float in `[-scale, scale)` by shifting the engine RNG around zero before scaling by the supplied magnitude.

functions.name: random_float_below
functions.description: Returns a uniform random float in `[0, upper_bound)` by scaling the engine RNG output with the supplied upper bound.

functions.name: initialize_sound_bank
functions.description: Registers one null-terminated table of shipped SFX paths into the shared runtime sound table by repeatedly calling `register_sound_sample` with each path and its normalization class.

functions.name: play_sound_effect_at_position
functions.description: Windows cRSound positional overload: folds a one-listener minimum-distance loop over GamePlayer[0]'s owned camera transform position, then plays one effect id with 25-unit attenuation and left-right pan derived from the supplied Vector3 reference.

functions.name: play_sound_effect
functions.description: Thin gameplay sound wrapper that forwards one effect id into the shared audio backend with the default volume, pitch, and pan parameters.

functions.name: play_sound_effect_scaled
functions.description: Thin gameplay sound wrapper that forwards one effect id and explicit gain into the shared audio backend, leaving pitch and pan on their default lanes.

functions.name: play_warning_sample_backend
functions.description: Warning-system wrapper that plays one sample id through the shared registered-sample backend and returns the live sample handle.

functions.name: stop_warning_sample_handle
functions.description: Warning-system wrapper that stops one live warning-sample handle through the shared registered-sample backend.

functions.name: initialize_sprite
functions.description: Initializes one sprite slot in the `cRSprite` runtime family before manager allocation links it into active/free lists.

functions.name: update_sprite
functions.description: Runs one live sprite's per-frame state update; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSprite::AI()`.

functions.name: register_sprite_texture
functions.description: Registers or reuses one sprite texture reference; iOS RSprite.o names this manager family `cRSpriteManager::Load(char*, int, int)` and also exposes the adjacent `LoadSet` variant.

functions.name: initialize_sprite_manager
functions.description: Initializes the global sprite manager, its sprite pool, and free-list heads; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::Init()`.

functions.name: kill_sprite
functions.description: Unlinks and returns one live sprite to the manager free list; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSprite::Kill()`.

functions.name: allocate_sprite
functions.description: Allocates one sprite from the manager free list, assigns texture ids, and links it into the selected active bucket; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::New(int, int, int, int)`.

functions.name: kill_game_sprites
functions.description: Kills all nonpersistent gameplay sprites while preserving manager state; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::KillGame()`.

functions.name: update_sprite_facing_angle
functions.description: Refreshes the sprite angle lane at `+0x7c` from motion delta in camera space, with the optional smoothing timer carried in `+0x8c/+0x90`. iOS RSprite.o names the matrix-driven tail/facing family `cRSprite::BuildTail(tMatrix*)`.

functions.name: set_sprite_manager_paused
functions.description: Writes the shared sprite-manager pause byte consumed by `update_sprite`, freezing or unfreezing sprite updates globally. iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::Pause(bool)`.

functions.name: set_sprite_texture_ref
functions.description: Assigns one sprite's texture reference and frame metadata from a manager texture id; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSprite::SetTextureRef(int, int)`.

functions.name: get_sprite_texture
functions.description: Returns the texture object for a manager texture id; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::GetTexture(int)`.

functions.name: get_sprite_texture_ref
functions.description: Returns the texture-ref record for a manager texture id; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::GetTextureRef(int)`.

functions.name: rstrcpy_checked_ascii
functions.description: Copies a null-terminated resource string to a destination buffer while reporting high-bit bytes through the engine's `Dodgy Rstrcpy` diagnostic. This helper is hot across path, text, and script setup code.

functions.name: find_case_insensitive_substring
functions.description: Parser-side metadata matcher used for case-insensitive, prefix-friendly tag probes.

functions.name: advance_to_next_crlf_line
functions.description: Resource-loader text helper that returns the first byte after the next CRLF pair, or null when there is no following non-empty line.

functions.name: strings_equal_case_insensitive_path
functions.description: ASCII case-insensitive equality comparator used by path, mesh, landscape, and music lookup helpers. Unlike the earlier parser comparator, both strings must terminate after the folded match.

functions.name: parse_next_signed_int
functions.description: Advances a text cursor to the next numeric token, parses an optional sign and decimal digits, and leaves the cursor after the consumed integer. Shared by shipped asset and level-script parsers.

functions.name: sample_tga_pixel_rgb
functions.description: Samples one bottom-up TGA-style pixel from the loaded font texture sheet buffer, expanding 8-bit grayscale pixels or returning packed 24-bit RGB.

functions.name: get_or_create_texture_ref
functions.description: Looks up or allocates one TextureRefList entry, using the 0xa4-byte TextureRef stride, 0x80-byte inline path, no-reuse flag, and slot-index/count bookkeeping behind sprite texture registration.

functions.name: attach_render_camera_source
functions.description: Attaches a source render object to one runtime camera/render slot and marks the slot active through flag bit 0.

functions.name: initialize_render_camera_slot
functions.description: Initializes one 0x28-byte runtime camera/render slot used by render_game_frame, seeding viewport lanes, draw-world state, and the default 0.8725f lane while leaving owner fields for later setup.

functions.name: initialize_translation_matrix
functions.description: Builds a 4x4 identity matrix with the supplied xyz translation in slots `+0x30/+0x34/+0x38`. `draw_sprite_quad` uses this helper to place one sprite quad in world space before submission.

functions.name: build_perspective_projection_matrix
functions.description: Builds the renderer's D3D-style perspective projection matrix from vertical FOV in radians, viewport aspect ratio, near plane, and far plane. Called by both `render_camera` and toon-outline setup.

functions.name: build_camera_view_matrix
functions.description: Builds the view matrix from camera eye, target, and up vectors by normalizing the look, right, and up axes and storing translated dot products. Called by `render_camera`.
